notes: SHIP v1.14.343 - Home card-surface unification (owner). CSS-only, #pg-cmd-scoped -> ?legacy byte-identical; joins the .340-.343 batch device-verify. Owner ask: make EVERY card on Home the same solid black as the 98 RACKS stat tile - the AI ASSISTANT card and the rack-line strip were lighter/blue-tinted; one shared surface token, applied everywhere, not per-card values; keep borders/radius, only the fill changes. Implementation: added ONE token `#pg-cmd{--rd-cardfill: linear-gradient(158deg,#050506 0%,#010102 46%,#000000 76%,#030304 100%)}` = the exact .stat (98 RACKS) tile fill, and pointed every Home card's `background` at it (borders/radius/box-shadows untouched). Converted 11 card surfaces: the blue-tinted ones that MISMATCHED -> .cc-asst (assistant, owner callout), .cc-qtools, .cc-sugg (were var(--surf-1) = blue 145deg gradient), .cc-rackline (owner callout, was rgba(92,242,255,.04)); plus the already-near-black ones folded onto the single token so there are no per-card literals left on Home -> .stat, .lens, .nba, .tile, .cc-agechip (were the identical stat literal - zero visual change), .cc-vital (was the shorter #050506->#000 76% gradient), .sigrow (was rgba(255,255,255,0.015)). Token is #pg-cmd-scoped ON PURPOSE so the other body.rd surfaces that share the same literal (body.rd .gx / .gx-bg / .rl-row / .rl-search-input = BUILD/rack-map chrome, NOT Home) keep their own fills and are untouched - no cross-surface blast radius. #rd-botnav (shared bottom nav) already carries this exact fill (.338) and is not a Home card - left as-is. Net visual: the assistant card + rack-line strip (and vitals/sigrow) go from blue-tinted to the stat-tile black; every Home card now reads as one uniform surface. Gates: CSS brace net-0 (1 token def reused the existing empty #pg-cmd{} rule + 11 background swaps, no rule count change), inline-JS vm.Script x3 (no JS changed), sw.js ok, version.json valid, three-stamp .342->.343; phantom-ship-gate + phantom-rd-reviewer pre-commit. DEVICE-VERIFY (HARD-REFRESH / clear SW first): on Home, the AI ASSISTANT card, the rack-line strip, QUICK TOOLS, NBA, and the RACKS/BLOCKERS/DEPLOYS stat tiles are ALL the same solid black - no blue-tinted card remains; borders/radius unchanged. ?legacy=1 pixel-identical. This is a batch item - verify alongside .340/.341/.342. RD-REVIEW FIX (P1, cascade, pre-commit): rd-reviewer caught that my per-card `background` swaps were SHADOWED by two later layers I hadn't checked - (1) a v1.14.310 SURFACE-GLOW `!important` block (~L51448) that is the REAL source-of-truth forcing .lens/.nba/.stat/.tile/.spill + BUILD .gx/.gx-bg/.gx-gold to the hardcoded stat literal, and (2) a machined-GLASS-SKIN rule (~L12064, `body.rd #pg-cmd .sigrow, .kpi, .nav-back-btn`, higher specificity) giving .sigrow a blue glass fill+edge. Net: .sigrow's swap was DEAD (still blue), and .lens/.stat/.nba/.tile were only coincidentally-correct via the !important literal (token was NOT their real source). Fix, done properly so the token is the GENUINE single source: moved the token def from #pg-cmd to `body.rd` (so it's in scope for the BUILD .gx selectors too), pointed the SURFACE-GLOW !important background at `var(--rd-cardfill)` (zero visual change - identical value - but now .lens/.nba/.stat/.tile/.spill + .gx family all single-source off the token), and added a dedicated `body.rd #pg-cmd .sigrow{background:var(--rd-cardfill);border:1px solid var(--cyan-border-lo)}` AFTER the glass skin (equal specificity, later source -> wins) so OPS SIGNAL rows actually go black. NOTE: .sigrow's border necessarily changed (the glass EDGE was baked into the multi-layer glass fill, so removing the blue fill removes that border) -> restored the standard cyan-lo hairline the other unified cards carry; radius/box-shadow untouched. body.rd token is still ?legacy-safe (legacy has no body.rd). BUILD .gx/.gx-bg/.gx-gold now reference the token (identical value, zero visual change) - the coupling already existed via the shared literal, just tokenized. NEXT = .344 assistant-card tap-to-open (restore openVaSheet door + CHAT chip), then desktop top-nav. ROLLBACK: revert this commit.
